description: Fun trivia queries regarding sports records, Oscar wins, classic movies, and legendary music.

questions.text: How many players does a soccer team have on the field at once during a match?
How often are the Olympic Games normally held (summer or winter)?
What color is the leader's jersey in the Tour de France bicycle race?
What is the official distance of a marathon race in kilometers?
Which country has won the most men's FIFA World Cups in total?
What is the maximum score in a single game of bowling (a perfect game)?
In which sport can you score a 'home run'?
How many pieces does a chess player start with at the beginning of a game?
How many holes does a standard golf course have?
Which legendary athlete has won the most Olympic gold medals in history?
Which character did Harrison Ford play in the Star Wars films?
What is the name of the fictional country where the Black Panther story is set?
Which 1997 film about a shipwreck won 11 Oscar awards?
Which famous artist played the role of Ally Campana in the film 'A Star Is Born' (2018)?
What is the name of the famous music event held annually in the UK (the world's largest greenfield festival)?
Who is considered the King of Pop?
In which animated film series do we meet the toys Woody and Buzz Lightyear?
What is the name of Apple's streaming service that produced the series Ted Lasso?
Which Swedish pop group won the Eurovision Song Contest in 1974 with the song Waterloo?
Which film won the very first Oscar for Best Picture in 1929?
How long is a tennis court in meters?
Which basketball player is widely known as 'Air Jordan'?
Which soccer club plays its home games at Old Trafford?
How many red balls do you start with in a game of Snooker?
Which board sport was added to the Olympic Games in Tokyo 2020?
Who is considered the most successful Formula 1 driver (most wins)?
Which German car manufacturer sponsors the football stadium of FC Bayern Munich?
Which artist released the ground-breaking album 'Thriller' in 1982?
What is the name of the famous Opera House in Australia?
Who is considered 'The Queen of Soul'?
What is the oldest tennis tournament in the world?
How many points do you get for a 'field goal' in American football?
Which cyclist won the Tour de France 5 times in a row (1991-1995)?
Which sport is played on ice with brooms and a heavy stone?
Who is considered the most winning female skier in Winter Olympics history?
What is the title of the highest-grossing film in history (adjusted for inflation)?
In what year was the streaming service Netflix founded?
Which song performed by Luis Fonsi and Daddy Yankee became a global hit in 2017?
What is the name of the famous game development studio that created the Grand Theft Auto and Red Dead Redemption games?
Which film directed by Bong Joon Ho became the first foreign language film to win the Oscar for Best Picture in 2020?
What breed of dog is Scooby-Doo?
Which actor voiced the Genie in Disney's original animated Aladdin (1992)?
Which instrument did Louis Armstrong play?
Who is the creator of the comic strip 'Peanuts' featuring Snoopy and Charlie Brown?
Which pop star has a loyal fanbase known as 'Swifties'?
In which city were the 2012 Summer Olympics held?
What is the name of the famous game console released by Nintendo in 2017 that can be used both handheld and docked?
Who wrote the epic fantasy novel series 'A Song of Ice and Fire' upon which the TV series Game of Thrones is based?
Which Marvel superhero group includes Iron Man, Thor, and Captain America?
What is the name of the character Keanu Reeves plays in the John Wick movies?

title: [EN] Arts, Literature & Mythology (Hard)
description: Explore questions regarding masterpiece paintings, classic novelists, and mythological tales.

questions.text: Which Norwegian artist painted the famous expressive work 'The Scream' in 1893?
Which author wrote the world-famous romantic tragedy 'Romeo and Juliet'?
Who is the most powerful god in Greek mythology, associated with thunder and the sky?
Which Renaissance painting by Leonardo da Vinci hanging in the Louvre shows a woman with a mysterious smile?
Who is the Norse god of thunder who rides across the sky in a chariot pulled by goats and wields the hammer Mjolnir?
Which British author wrote the fantasy success about the wizard boy Harry Potter?
Who is the ruler of the underworld in Greek mythology?
What is the name of Captain Nemo's ship in Jules Verne's classic 'Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea'?
Which famous Norwegian playwright wrote the socially critical play 'A Doll's House' in 1879?
Which Renaissance artist painted the grand ceiling of the Sistine Chapel in the Vatican?
Which region do the myths about the gods Odin, Thor, and Loki originate from?
Which Dutch post-impressionist artist painted 'The Starry Night' and cut off part of his own ear?
Which author wrote the epic fantasy series 'The Lord of the Rings'?
Who is the goddess of beauty and love in Roman mythology (corresponding to Greek Aphrodite)?
Which novel by Herman Melville is about the hunt for the great white whale?
What is the name of the central location in Dante Alighieri's 'Divine Comedy' where the lost souls meet Lucifer?
Which Spanish surrealist artist painted 'The Persistence of Memory' featuring melting clocks?
Which author wrote the novel 'Don Quixote', considered the first modern novel?
Who is the Egyptian god of the sun and creation, often depicted with a falcon head?
Which French author wrote historical classics such as 'The Three Musketeers' and 'The Count of Monte Cristo'?
Which famous monument in Agra, India, was built by Emperor Shah Jahan as a mausoleum for his wife?
Which era in European art history literally translates to 'rebirth'?
What is the name of the famous bronze sculpture by Auguste Rodin showing a man in deep thought?
Who is the goddess of wisdom and war in Greek mythology, who gave her name to a major Greek city?
Which famous novel by Mary Shelley is considered one of the earliest works of science fiction?
What was the name of the Trojan prince who triggered the Trojan War by abducting Helen?
Which author wrote the novel series 'The Legend of the Ice People'?
Which instrument was Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart famous for mastering as a child prodigy?
What is the name of Odin's eight-legged horse in Norse mythology?
Which German composer continued to create grand music even after becoming completely deaf?
What is the name of King Arthur's famous sword, which he pulled from a stone?
Which Russian author wrote the giant novels 'War and Peace' and 'Anna Karenina'?
Who is the Roman god of war (corresponding to Greek Ares)?
Which French impressionist painter is famous for his many depictions of water lilies in his garden at Giverny?
Which author wrote the novel '1984' warning against totalitarian surveillance?
What were the names of Odin's ravens that flew across the world to gather information?
Which Spanish cubist painter created the monumental anti-war painting 'Guernica' in 1937?
Which American author is considered the master of detective and horror fiction and wrote 'The Raven'?
What is the name of the realm of giants in Norse mythology?
Which German poet and scientist wrote the world-famous tragedy 'Faust'?
Who wrote the novel 'Around the World in Eighty Days'?
Which author wrote the 1897 gothic horror novel 'Dracula'?
Who is the Greek goddess of love and beauty?
Who wrote the book 'The Little Prince'?
Which German poet wrote the poem 'Ode to Joy', used in Beethoven's 9th Symphony?
Who painted the famous artwork 'Girl with a Pearl Earring'?
What is the name of the Norse god of fertility, peace, and sunshine, who is the brother of Freya?
Who wrote the classic epic poems 'Odyssey' and 'Iliad'?
Which French sculptor designed the famous Statue of Liberty (given to the US)?
Who is the goddess of wisdom in Roman mythology (corresponding to Greek Athena)?
